title: New Drug Offers Hope for Pancreatic Cancer Patients
subtitle: While not a cure, a new treatment doubles survival time for those with a difficult disease.

summary.en: Medical researchers have shared positive news regarding a new drug for pancreatic cancer. The drug, called daraxonrasib, has shown the ability to double survival times in patients. Experts describe the development as a significant clinical breakthrough. However, doctors warn that the disease remains very dangerous and difficult to treat. Further research continues as scientists look for ways to improve long-term outcomes.
summary.zh: 醫學研究人員分享了關於一種胰臟癌新藥的正面消息。該藥物名為 daraxonrasib，已展現出能將患者存活時間延長一倍的能力。專家形容此發展為一項重大的臨床突破。然而，醫師提醒該疾病依然非常危險且難以治療。隨著科學家尋求改善長期預後的方法，相關研究仍持續進行中。

paragraphs.en: For many years, a diagnosis of pancreatic cancer has been seen as a very serious situation with few treatment options. Because the pancreas is located deep inside the body, the disease often goes unnoticed until it is in an advanced stage. However, recent reports from the medical community suggest that a new drug could change how doctors manage this illness. According to Deutsche Welle (DW), a new medicine called daraxonrasib has shown promising results in a recent study, effectively doubling the survival time for patients.
paragraphs.zh: 多年來，胰臟癌的診斷一直被視為極其嚴重的狀況，且治療選擇有限。由於胰臟位於人體深處，這種疾病往往在發展到晚期之前都難以察覺。然而，醫學界近期的報告顯示，一種新藥或許能改變醫師處理此疾病的方式。根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）報導，一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥在近期研究中展現了令人期待的成果，能有效將患者的存活時間延長一倍。

paragraphs.en: Pancreatic cancer is known for being notoriously difficult to treat. Most patients live for only a few months after their initial diagnosis. In the new study, participants who took daraxonrasib lived for a median of 13.2 months, compared to only 6.6 months for those who received standard chemotherapy. Beyond living longer, patients also reported a better quality of life and experienced fewer severe side effects than those on traditional treatments. Experts believe this is one of the most important developments in the field of metastatic pancreatic cancer in many years.
paragraphs.zh: 胰臟癌以極難治療著稱。大多數患者在初次確診後僅能存活幾個月。在這項新研究中，服用 daraxonrasib 的受試者中位存活期為十三點二個月，而接受標準化學治療的患者僅有六點六個月。除了存活時間更長，患者也表示生活品質有所提升，且相較於接受傳統治療者，出現嚴重副作用的情況較少。專家認為，這是多年來在轉移性胰臟癌領域中最重要的進展之一。

paragraphs.en: The reason for this success lies in how the drug works. About 90% of people with this type of cancer carry a mutation in a specific gene called the RAS gene. This gene produces a protein that forces cells to grow and divide uncontrollably, which leads to the formation of tumors. Daraxonrasib is designed to target this specific gene, stopping the protein from helping the cancer grow. Dieter Saur, a professor at the Technical University of Munich, described the development as a revolutionary breakthrough for patients.
paragraphs.zh: 這項成功的關鍵在於該藥物的運作機制。約百分之九十的此類癌症患者帶有一種稱為 RAS 基因的特定基因突變。該基因會產生一種蛋白質，迫使細胞不受控制地生長與分裂，進而形成腫瘤。Daraxonrasib 的設計旨在針對此特定基因，阻止該蛋白質協助癌細胞生長。慕尼黑工業大學（Technical University of Munich）教授迪特·紹爾（Dieter Saur）形容這項發展對患者而言是一項革命性的突破。

paragraphs.en: Despite this excitement, medical professionals are careful to manage expectations. Susanne Weg-Remers, a physician at the German Cancer Research Center, noted that while the results are promising, the drug is not a cure. In most cases, the disease remains a fatal condition. Even with the best available treatments, including surgery and chemotherapy, only about 11% of patients are still alive five years after their diagnosis. Because the cancer is so hard to detect early, doctors do not currently recommend population-wide screening, as existing methods are not effective enough for such a deep-seated organ.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管各界感到振奮，醫療專業人員仍謹慎地管理大眾的期望。德國癌症研究中心（German Cancer Research Center）醫師蘇珊·韋格-雷默斯（Susanne Weg-Remers）指出，雖然結果令人期待，但該藥物並非治癒良方。在大多數情況下，此疾病仍屬致命。即使採取現有最佳治療方式，包括手術與化學治療，僅約百分之十一的患者在確診後能存活五年。由於此癌症極難早期發現，醫師目前並不建議進行全面篩檢，因為現有方法對於這種位於深層器官的癌症效果並不顯著。

paragraphs.en: This news comes as part of a wider conversation about global cancer care. At a recent summit in Chicago attended by over 40,000 experts, researchers discussed the ongoing struggle to eradicate the disease. According to The Guardian, while survival rates for many cancers have improved significantly over the last few decades, progress remains uneven across the world. The World Health Organization reports that more than 50 million people are currently living after a cancer diagnosis, but hundreds of thousands still die each year from forms of the disease that could be treated if caught earlier.
paragraphs.zh: 這則新聞是全球癌症照護廣泛討論的一部分。在芝加哥近期一場有超過四萬名專家參與的峰會上，研究人員討論了根除此疾病的持續抗爭。根據衛報（The Guardian）報導，雖然過去幾十年來許多癌症的存活率已顯著提升，但全球各地的進展仍不均衡。世界衛生組織（World Health Organization）指出，目前有超過五千萬人是在確診癌症後存活下來，但每年仍有數十萬人死於若能及早發現便可治療的癌症類型。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the medical community is exploring several paths to improve patient outcomes. In addition to new drugs like daraxonrasib, researchers are investigating the use of mRNA vaccines. These vaccines are designed to help the immune system recognize and prevent cancer from returning after surgery. While these technologies are still in the testing phase, they represent a shift toward more personalized and effective treatments.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，醫學界正探索多種途徑以改善患者的預後。除了像 daraxonrasib 這樣的新藥外，研究人員也正在調查信使核糖核酸（mRNA）疫苗的應用。這些疫苗旨在協助免疫系統辨識並預防癌症在手術後復發。雖然這些技術仍處於測試階段，但它們代表了朝向更個人化且更有效治療方式的轉變。

paragraphs.en: For now, the focus remains on combining existing methods with these new breakthroughs. Surgeons continue to remove tumors when possible, followed by chemotherapy and radiation. However, the success of daraxonrasib provides a new tool for doctors to extend the lives of their patients. As research continues, the goal is to move from simply managing the disease to finding more permanent solutions. For families affected by this diagnosis, these incremental steps in science offer a small but important light of hope in a very difficult journey.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，重點仍放在將現有方法與這些新突破相結合。外科醫師在可能的情況下持續切除腫瘤，隨後輔以化學治療與放射治療。然而，daraxonrasib 的成功為醫師提供了一項延長患者生命的新工具。隨著研究持續進行，目標是從單純的疾病管理轉向尋找更永久的解決方案。對於受此診斷影響的家庭而言，這些科學上的漸進式進展，在艱難的抗癌旅程中提供了一道微小但重要的希望之光。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To control or treat a medical condition over a period of time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「管理」或「設法完成」，這裡指醫療上的病情控制。文中：However, recent reports from the medical community suggest that a new drug could change how doctors manage this illness.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To possess or have a specific gene or disease within one's body.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「攜帶」物品，這裡指體內擁有某種基因。文中：About 90% of people with this type of cancer carry a mutation in a specific gene called the RAS gene.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To keep something under control or to moderate one's hopes or reactions.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指調節人們的心理預期，不要過度樂觀。文中：Despite this excitement, medical professionals are careful to manage expectations.

sources.title: Pancreatic cancer: New drug extends lives but isn't a cure

previousArticles.title: New Hope in the Fight Against Pancreatic Cancer
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers are celebrating a significant development in treating pancreatic cancer. A new drug called daraxonrasib has been shown to double survival times in patients. While this is a major step forward, experts warn that the disease remains extremely difficult to defeat. Pharmaceutical companies are now investing heavily in this area of research. Despite these advances, doctors emphasize that early detection and prevention are still the best tools for saving lives.

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope for Cancer Patients
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. Scientists have developed new drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for some patients. Additionally, hospitals are adopting a new, non-invasive urine test for bladder cancer. These developments represent a shift toward more manageable and less painful care.

previousArticles.title: New Medical Breakthroughs Offer Fresh Hope in Cancer Fight
previousArticles.summaryEn: Medical researchers have recently shared exciting updates on new cancer treatments at a major conference in Chicago. These new drugs work by helping the immune system find and destroy cancer cells that were previously hidden. One specific pill has shown the ability to double survival times for patients with pancreatic cancer. Another treatment helps patients with various common cancers by removing the 'invisibility cloaks' that tumors use to hide. While these results are early, experts believe they represent a significant step forward in oncology.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason that pancreatic cancer is frequently discovered at an advanced stage?

options.A: It is resistant to all forms of standard chemotherapy.
options.B: The location of the pancreas makes it difficult to detect early.
options.C: Patients rarely show symptoms until the RAS gene mutation occurs.
options.D: Doctors do not have enough experience to identify the initial signs.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第一段明確指出「Because the pancreas is located deep inside the body, the disease often goes unnoticed until it is in an advanced stage」，直接說明了器官位置深層是導致診斷延遲的主因。選項 A 雖然提到化療，但文章並未說這是發現晚的原因；選項 C 的基因突變是致病機制，而非診斷困難的原因；選項 D 則完全未在文中提及，屬於無中生有的選項。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what can be inferred about the future of pancreatic cancer treatment?

options.A: Daraxonrasib will soon replace the need for surgery and radiation.
options.B: Medical experts believe that a universal cure will be available within five years.
options.C: Treatment strategies are shifting toward a combination of targeted drugs and existing therapies.
options.D: mRNA vaccines are currently the most effective treatment for patients with metastatic cancer.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章最後一段提到「the focus remains on combining existing methods with these new breakthroughs」，且文中提到手術、化療與新藥 daraxonrasib 的併用，顯示治療策略正朝向整合性發展。選項 A 錯誤，因為文中並未說新藥會取代手術；選項 B 過度樂觀，文中專家強調這並非治癒；選項 D 錯誤，因為 mRNA 疫苗目前仍處於測試階段，並非現行最有效的治療手段。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'manage' mean in the phrase 'manage expectations'?

options.A: To control or limit the level of hope or anticipation.
options.B: To organize the logistics of a medical appointment.
options.C: To successfully cure a patient of a difficult disease.
options.D: To increase the amount of information given to the public.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境下，「manage expectations」意指「控制或調節期望值」，避免人們因為新藥的出現而過度樂觀，忽略了這並非完全治癒的事實。選項 B 將 manage 解釋為管理行程，與語境不符；選項 C 將其誤解為治療疾病，這與文中提到的「not a cure」矛盾；選項 D 則與控制期望值的目的背道而馳。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main message of the article?

options.A: Pancreatic cancer is no longer a fatal disease due to recent breakthroughs.
options.B: The RAS gene mutation is the only cause of pancreatic cancer in the modern world.
options.C: While new treatments like daraxonrasib offer hope, pancreatic cancer remains a complex challenge.
options.D: Global cancer summits have proven that early screening is the only way to save lives.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文結構平衡，既介紹了 daraxonrasib 帶來的醫療突破與生存期延長，同時也透過專家觀點提醒讀者該病仍具致命性且治療困難，總結了科學進步與現實挑戰並存的現況。選項 A 過度誇大，與文中「not a cure」衝突；選項 B 將 90% 的患者比例誤解為「唯一原因」；選項 D 雖然提到篩檢，但文章指出目前篩檢方法尚不成熟，並非唯一解方。
